title: Latin Wednesdays at Tongue & Groove
location: Tongue & Groove, 565 Main St NE, Atlanta, GA
description: Weekly Latin night in one of Atlanta's most intimate dance clubs — salsa, bachata, reggaetón with a great crowd. Perfect for couples who want to dance close, take a lesson together, or just people-watch from the lounge.

title: Summer Slamajama: A Summer Gallery Opening
location: Anne Irwin Fine Art, 690 Miami Cir NE, Atlanta, GA
description: Open-air summer art opening with a DJ, refreshments, and rotating new works. A relaxed, walk-around kind of date — easy conversation starter and a chance to pick out something for the apartment together.

title: Na'im Lynn at Helium Comedy Club
location: Helium Comedy Club, 3070 Windward Plaza, Alpharetta, GA
description: A polished headliner in a small-room club setting — exactly the right vibe for a first date or a 'let's get out of the house' weeknight. Cheap enough to grab dinner before or drinks after.

title: Legacy Wine Tasting at Norwood Vineyard
location: Norwood Vineyard & Gardens, 2441 Highway 138 SE, Jonesboro, GA
description: Guided Southern wine tasting on a quiet vineyard just south of Atlanta. Stargaze after, share a bottle — the kind of slow, romantic night the city never quite delivers.

title: ATLANTA'S AFRO CARIBBEAN FEST
location: Embr Lounge, 1136 Crescent Ave NE, Atlanta, GA
price: Free entry before 5 PM w/ RSVP (21+)
description: Indoor + outdoor dancefloor with DJs spinning 90s/00s reggae, Afrobeats, and R&B. Late afternoon energy, daylight dancing, and easy vibes — couples who want to move together without the late-night club scene.

title: The Dinner Detective True Crime Murder Mystery Dinner
location: Sonesta Atlanta Northwest, 2265 Kirkwood Creek Cove, Smyrna, GA
description: Interactive murder mystery over a four-course dinner — you and your date are part of the show. Built-in conversation, built-in laughter, and a story you'll talk about for weeks.

title: Vinnie D. & Co. — Better Get It in Your Soul (Jazz)
location: Red Light Cafe, 553-1 Amsterdam Ave NE, Atlanta, GA
description: Soul jazz tribute in Atlanta's classic listening room. Intimate, low-key, candle-and-wine energy — the kind of date that makes you slow down and actually listen to each other.

title: Suzie with a Z: Suzie Toot One Woman Show
location: City Winery Atlanta, 650 North Ave NE, Atlanta, GA
description: Campy, irreverent one-woman show paired with City Winery's wine-and-dinner setup. Eat, drink, laugh — and have a built-in 'remember when' moment from the same night.

title: NE-YO & AKON: Nights Like This Tour 2026
location: Lakewood Amphitheatre, 2002 Lakewood Way, Atlanta, GA
description: Two R&B heavyweights on one stage outdoors. Pack a blanket, sing every word, and turn it into a late-night drive home singing the same songs.

title: Scene on the Green: Zootopia 2 (Outdoor Movie)
location: The Art Place at Mountain View, 3330 Sandy Plains Rd, Marietta, GA
description: Bring blankets and a picnic — an outdoor family-friendly screening on the lawn. Cheap, no-pressure, and a low-stakes weeknight date that ends before midnight.

title: Prima Materia — Group Exhibition Opening
time: 11:00 AM (opening day; runs through the month)
location: Empire Arts Gallery, Atlanta, GA
description: Group exhibition exploring the sacred origin of the creative act. Walk through together, talk about what you each see, and grab coffee after — a low-key afternoon date in the city.

title: ATL Vegan Festival
location: Piedmont Park, 1332 Monroe Dr NE, Atlanta, GA
description: Plant-based food festival in the heart of Piedmont Park — graze vegan vendors, people-watch under the trees, and save room for dessert. Sunny Sunday energy, easy to dress for, easy to leave when you've had enough.

title: PeachFest 2026
location: Underground Atlanta, 50 Upper Alabama St, Atlanta, GA
description: A celebration of Georgia's most iconic fruit — peach-inspired dishes, cocktails, and live energy in a historic venue. A splurgy Sunday date that doubles as a culinary adventure.

title: LA PLACITA — Dancing Under the Stars
location: Atlanta (outdoor venue — check listing)
description: Open-air Latin social under the night sky. Beginner-friendly vibes, music, and food — perfect for couples who want to dance without committing to a full club night.

title: Life Space Vegan Chef Cook-Off
location: 294 Hamilton E Holmes Dr NW, Atlanta, GA
description: Live plant-based cook-off with local chefs competing in real time. Watch, taste, and vote — a community-driven food event that turns a normal Sunday into something memorable.

title: Sunday Wine Tasting & Pizza (Couples Meetup)
location: North Atlanta (hosted location via Meetup)
description: Hosted couples event — relaxed afternoon of wine tasting paired with pizza. Low-pressure, no awkwardness — built-in social structure for couples looking to meet other couples.

title: Poetic Jazz: Prince Tribute
location: The Vick at Healey, 57 Forsyth St NW #49, Atlanta, GA
description: Live reinterpretation of Prince's catalog through a jazz lens. Intimate downtown setting — the kind of 'we just happened to find this' date that ends up being the one you both remember.

title: Signature Saturdays at Tongue & Groove
location: Tongue & Groove, 565 Main St NE, Atlanta, GA
description: Atlanta's signature Saturday-night party — open format, crowd is dressed up, the lounge and dancefloor are both buzzing. A dressy, big-night-out date with great food options in walking distance.

title: Phillippia and Yoshi (R&B, Dinner & a Show)
location: Perfect Note Atlanta, 1100 Peachtree St NE, Atlanta, GA
description: R&B showcase with an optional dinner pairing — couples can turn the whole evening into one reservation. Smooth vocals, table service, midtown views.

title: Margaritaville: Jimmy Buffett's Coral Reefer Band
location: Synovus Bank Amphitheater at Chastain Park, Atlanta, GA
description: Bring a picnic and a bottle of wine to Chastain Park for a sunset, summer-easy night of Buffett singalongs. Couples who grew up on 'Margaritaville' will love it — couples new to it will have a great time anyway.

title: Savanna Nights at Zoo Atlanta — Boy Bands & Girl Groups
location: Zoo Atlanta, 800 Cherokee Ave SE, Atlanta, GA
description: Adults-only night at the zoo with a Y2K/throwback soundtrack, cocktails, and a totally different energy than a daytime visit. One of Atlanta's most original date nights — slow walking, animal energy, and nostalgia.

title: Karlous Miller at Helium Comedy Club
location: Helium Comedy Club, 3070 Windward Plaza, Alpharetta, GA
description: 85 South / 'Wild 'N Out' favorite headlining a full weekend. The kind of loud, big-room laugh that ends with drinks afterward — easy to pair with dinner in Alpharetta.

title: Friday Night Live — 404 Theory (Modern Jazz Trio)
location: The Reading Room, Atlanta, GA
description: Local modern jazz trio with a rotating guest — a low-key Friday listening-room date. Grab dinner first in the neighborhood, then settle in for music that rewards attention.

title: Lionel Richie & Earth, Wind & Fire — Sing a Song All Night Long
location: State Farm Arena, 1 State Farm Dr, Atlanta, GA
description: Two legends, one stage, all the songs. Date-night energy dialed to 11 — both of you will know every chorus. The show that turns into the long, singing-in-the-car drive home.

title: INNER VIEWS Art Exhibition — Opening
location: Atlanta (ARTSATL listed venue)
description: Collective exhibition from fourteen contemporary artists musing on home, family, and community. The opening weekend is the best time to go — full energy, artists often in the room, and the conversations it sparks last longer than the visit.
